perturbation.original_text: 1.13 "Parts" shall mean parts, components, subassemblies and other materials used by MSL to fulfill orders for IBM. Parts shall also include the following: (a) IBM Parts are those Parts which are purchased by MSL from IBM,. (b) IBM Designated Parts are those Parts purchased by MSL from IBM nominated suppliers, (c) IBM Consigned Parts are those Parts owned by IBM or IBM customers which are consigned to MSL, and (d) MSL Procured Parts are those Parts which are directly procured by MSL and are other than IBM Parts or IBM Designated Parts.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.13 "Parts" shall mean parts, components, subassemblies and other materials used by MSL to fulfill orders for IBM. Parts shall also include the following: (a) IBM Parts are those Parts which are purchased by MSL from IBM,. (b) IBM Designated Parts are those Parts purchased by MSL from IBM nominated suppliers, and (d) MSL Procured Parts are those Parts which are directly procured by MSL and are other than IBM Parts or IBM Designated Parts.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the definition of 'IBM Consigned Parts,' which are 'those Parts owned by IBM or IBM customers which are consigned to MSL,' the contract creates ambiguity regarding the handling and ownership of these consigned parts. This omission creates a contradiction because later sections, such as Section 5.1(c), refer to MSL's responsibilities regarding consigned products, but the definition of what constitutes IBM Consigned Parts is missing, thereby creating in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: c) notice of the inability of the other Party to perform due to the existence of a Force Majeure event, as described in Section 16.17 of this Agreement, which is reasonably determined by the terminating Party to be a continuing condition. Provided, however, that no such termination under this section after the Effective Date shall operate to rescind the transfer of the assets, as listed in Attachment 3: Asset List, unless IBM terminates this Agreement pursuant to Section 5.0 for MSL's failure to pay for such assets, in which case MSL shall return, at its cost, all such assets in MSL's possession at termination.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'IBM Consigned Parts', which are referenced in other sections like the contradicted text. By omitting this definition, the changed text creates ambiguity and a direct conflict because the contract now lacks clarity on MSL's responsibilities regarding these consigned products, as outlined in the contradicted section. This fulfills the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition because essential language was removed in one section, creating a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections.

perturbation.justification: The omission of the definition for element '4' in the Total Manufacturing Cost (TMC) formula within section 7.2 directly contradicts the pricing formula outlined in the 'Contradicted Text'. The Contradicted Text refers to Section 7.2 for the cost of [*], which now lacks a complete definition due to the removal of '4'. Because the formula to determine P=A+B+C+D in the 'Contradicted Text' relies on components which are defined in 7.2, removing one of those components makes the entire pricing structure unclear, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 19.5 Returned Product Turn Around Time MSL shall set an objective to complete Failure Analysis, repair or replacement of defective Products, within [*] Days after receipt from IBM. Upon or before the [*] Day, MSL will ship the repaired or replaced Product, to IBM at MSL's expense. If repair or replacement is not possible, MSL will refund to IBM, MSL's price associated with the failed Product, that is under warranty and the price paid by IBM to MSL or any third parties or the intercompany transfer price for IBM Parts, for all Parts, if the price of such Parts were not included in MSL's price.
perturbation.changed_text: 19.5 Returned Product Turn Around Time MSL shall set an objective to complete Failure Analysis, repair or replacement of defective Products, within [*] Days after receipt from IBM. Upon or before the [*] Day, MSL will ship the repaired or replaced Product, to IBM at MSL's expense. If repair or replacement is not possible, MSL will refund to IBM, MSL's price associated with the failed Product, that is under warranty and the price paid by IBM to MSL or any third parties.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'or the intercompany transfer price for IBM Parts, for all Parts, if the price of such Parts were not included in MSL's price' from the conditions where repair or replacement of the product is not possible, that is under warranty, it is no longer clear how or if the intercompany transfer price will be paid for. This will become a point of contention in the case of a warranty, since the text no longer specifies that all parts will be refunded for, creating ambiguity in enforcement and application of the paragraph.
perturbation.contradicted_text: a) MSL expressly warrants that all Products, MSL supplied materials and Parts, and work prepared for IBM will conform in all material respects to the specifications, drawings, or other descriptions furnished or adopted by IBM, and will be of specified quality, good workmanship, and free from defect subject to the following terms: i) MSL's warranty for IBM Designated Parts will be as long as, and will be on the same terms and conditions as the Parts supplier's warranty stated in the relevant purchasing contract. ii) MSL will not provide a warranty for IBM Parts. However, MSL will provide Failure Analysis for such parts. MSL will process such failed Parts as "Return to MSL for credit to IBM" in a timely manner. iii) MSL will not provide a warranty for consigned Products. However, MSL will provide failure analysis for such Products. MSL will process such failed Products per IBM's instruction in a timely manor. iv) MSL will warrant all MSL Procured Parts for [*] months unless otherwise stated in the applicable Product Attachment, or agreed to by IBM in writing. v) MSL will warrant its workmanship for [*] months unless other stated in the applicable Product Attachment.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 19.5 specifies that if repair or replacement is not possible, MSL will refund IBM's price for the failed product, including the intercompany transfer price for IBM Parts, if the price of such Parts were not included in MSL's price. The changed text omits "or the intercompany transfer price for IBM Parts, for all Parts, if the price of such Parts were not included in MSL's price." This omission contradicts the warranty terms outlined in section a), which imply that MSL is responsible for MSL supplied materials and Parts conforming to IBM's specifications. By removing the specific provision for refunding intercompany transfer prices for parts, the changed text creates uncertainty and potential conflict regarding the financial responsibility for defective parts covered under warranty, thus creating a contradiction.
